meaning: v.重新计划,重订...的时间表
examples: Since I'll be away, I'd like to reschedule the meeting.
I'm assuming you've tried to reschedule your rehearsals.
Cargo aircraft, in contrast, might be easier to reschedule, as might routine military flights.
Why not reschedule it for the spring and hold two marathons in one year?
The bill's main sponsor, Rep. Smith, said Tuesday he plans to reschedule the hearing in February.
